Empires and Puzzles Ribbit Review: The Frog Kingdom’s Heavy Hitter

Don't let the goofy grin fool you. Ribbit packs a punch, cuts mana, and poisons his way through 3-star tournaments. We dive into why this Mighty Pet is still a top pick for Rush Attack and Bloody Battle modes.
Ribbit Review: Why This Frog Still Kicks in 3-Star Tournaments
Ribbit: The Gladiator of Frog Kingdom (And Your 3-Star Rush MVP)
Meet Ribbit, the sword-wielding, beer-chugging frog who proves you don't need to be a prince to be useful. Released back in November 2022 as part of the

family, this 3-star Holy Monk quickly established himself as a staple in competitive 3-star play. While he might look like he belongs in a fairytale, his kit is strictly business: punishing damage, mana control, and damage over time.

He’s a classic hit-3 hero who thrives in fast-paced environments. If you’re looking for a hero to shut down the wings in a Rush tournament or add some toxicity (the literal kind) to your Bloody Battle defense, Ribbit is your amphibian.

Let’s take a moment to appreciate the art. It’s a bipedal frog in a tunic, wielding a short sword and—crucially—holding a frothy wooden tankard. He’s clearly just left the tavern to defend his swamp. The "Gladiator of Frog Kingdom" title feels slightly grandiose for a guy who looks like he's one drink away from toppling over, but that’s the Mighty Pets charm. The vibrant colors and whimsical design are a nice break from the edgy, skull-clad warriors we usually get.
When we look at Ribbit's stats through the lens of 2026, he’s definitely showing his age.
Respectable for 2022, but modern hitters like
(559) have left him in the dust.
Solid, allowing him to take a few hits.
This is the main pain point. New 3-stars like
are pushing nearly 1000 HP out of the box. Ribbit is squishy by modern standards and relies heavily on emblems and limit breaking to survive.
Despite the "old" stats, his distribution is balanced enough that he doesn't feel like a glass cannon, provided you invest in him.
This skill is the reason you level Ribbit. It’s a triple threat that provides immense utility in 3-star arenas:
Hitting three targets for 250% is excellent. It softens up the enemy lines significantly.
This is the crown jewel. In
tournaments, cutting mana by 15% can prevent three enemies from firing for an entire turn. It breaks the rhythm of the match and buys you precious time.
While 65 damage per turn doesn't sound like a nuke, in
(where healing is disabled), every point of permanent damage counts. It also helps finish off low-HP survivors hiding behind riposte or defense buffs.
In Rush Attack tournaments, he becomes Very Fast (6.5 tiles), making that mana cut devastatingly frequent.
. That is high efficiency for a 3-star special.
-15%. This is a "silent" silence. It effectively removes about 1.5 to 2 tiles' worth of mana from the enemy, often forcing them to make an extra match to charge.

family offers excellent synergy with minion summoners, making Ribbit a natural partner for heroes like

The stats boost for minions is nice, but the real benefit is the damage mitigation from Fiends. In 3-star tournaments, fiends are rare, but when they do appear, this bonus helps Ribbit shrug them off slightly faster.
every time an ally summons a minion makes him surprisingly durable and fast if paired correctly. With
(who summons minions for everyone), Ribbit can passively generate mana and heal himself every turn.
Dealing 85% damage to all enemies when they summon a fiend is situational in 3-star play, but hilarious when it triggers.
is simple and effective. Since Ribbit is a damage dealer, a raw 20% boost to his Attack stat for the first 6 turns helps him keep up with the power creep of newer heroes.
Upon release, Ribbit was immediately recognized as a "better Bauchan" (the Avalon 3-star). He offered similar mana control but with much higher damage output.

has not. Mana cut is a mechanic that does not age. -15% mana is just as effective against a 1000 HP hero as it is against a 700 HP hero.

Hitting 3 enemies with a mana cut is devastating in Rush wars.

The key to unlocking Ribbit is
. His passive allows him to gain free mana and health, effectively making him faster and tankier.
Kvasir keeps the minions flowing, triggering Ribbit's passive constantly. Ribbit cuts mana, and Faiez overheals. Note: Be careful of the poison overwrite!
Complete mana denial. Treevil slows them down, Bauchan cuts mana, and Ribbit cuts mana. The enemy will never fire.

Heroes like Helo can wipe his poison.
As mentioned, Kvasir's bees inflict poison. If Ribbit fires
the bees hit, he overwrites their (usually weaker) poison with his strong one (good). If bees hit
Ribbit, they overwrite his strong poison with weak bee poison (bad).
Ribbit is a fantastic pull. Level him immediately. He will be your best friend in tournaments and can help you punch above your weight class by denying enemy specials.
You likely have newer, shinier toys like the Construct heroes. However, keep at least one Ribbit for

. His specific combination of Hit-3 and Mana Cut is still rare and valuable. If you have the Aethers to spare, a Limit Break 1 is recommended to fix his stats, but save your Alpha Aethers for 5-star heroes unless you are ultra-competitive in 3-star tournaments.
The community has always had a soft spot for this tipsy frog.
“Good to pair with minion summoners Kvasir and Dawn I think.”
— A spot-on observation from the forums.
— The general consensus upon his release.
“You nerfed Ribbit so bad it is more like a bait and switch!”
— Some players were unhappy with adjustments post-beta, a classic E&P tale.

Ribbit is the perfect example of a hero who ages gracefully because utility never goes out of style. He might not have the sky-high stats of the latest releases, but when you need to shut down an enemy flank in a Rush tournament, this frog is still your prince charming. Just maybe don't let him drive the chariot after he finishes that tankard.
A solid keeper for tournament enthusiasts.
Is Ribbit worth leveling? A detailed review of the 3-star Holy hero from the Mighty Pets family, analyzing his stats, special skill, and viability in modern tournaments.
